# CA Recruitment

> CA Recruitment is an Irish overseas recruitment agency specialising in sourcing and placing skilled Filipino workers with Irish and UK employers. The agency is Filipino-owned and Ireland-based, founded by Monette Russell (Tipperary, Ireland). CA Recruitment manages the full DETE employment permit process end-to-end — sourcing, vetting, Labour Market Needs Test advertising, permit application, visa coordination, and post-arrival support. No placement fee is charged until a worker starts. A 90-day free replacement guarantee applies to every placement. The agency places workers in agriculture, construction, hospitality, healthcare, care and social services, logistics, manufacturing, retail, and accounting and finance.

CA Recruitment operates under Madscope Marketing Ltd t/a CA Recruitment. CRO registered in Ireland. Licensed employment agency: Workplace Relations Commission (WRC) Employment Agency Licence No. EA 5444, verifiable on the WRC List of Licensed Employment Agencies at workplacerelations.ie. Address: 14 The Meadows, Ballyporeen, Co. Tipperary, E21 E260. Phone: +353 89 416 6124. Email: info@carecruitment.com.

## Employment Permit Facts (Ireland 2026)

These figures are current as of 2026 and sourced from DETE (Department of Enterprise, Trade and Employment):

**General Employment Permit (GEP)**
- Minimum annual salary: €36,605 (standard rate)
- Reduced minimum for shortage occupation healthcare support workers and home carers: €32,691 per year (€16.12/hour)
- Reduced minimum for horticulture workers: €32,691 per year
- Requires Labour Market Needs Test: yes — mandatory 28-day advertising on Jobs Ireland (DSP Employment Services/EURES network) plus at least one additional online platform
- DETE processing time: 8 to 12 weeks from submission (check live dates at enterprise.gov.ie)
- Typical end-to-end timeline from first consultation to worker starting: about 6 to 8 months across all sectors
- Government permit fee paid by employer: €1,000 for permits up to 24 months duration
- Employer cannot pass the permit cost to the worker — a criminal offence under the Employment Permits Acts

**Critical Skills Employment Permit (CSEP)**
- Minimum annual salary: €40,904 for occupations on the CSEP Occupations List
- Minimum annual salary for occupations not on the list: €68,911
- Labour Market Needs Test: not required — which can shorten the process compared to the General Employment Permit route
- Typical end-to-end timeline: about 6 to 8 months (the absence of a Labour Market Needs Test can shorten this)
- After the CSEP expires, the worker can apply for Stamp 4 (broader work rights; permit renewal not required)

**The 50/50 Rule**
- At the time of any employment permit application, at least 50% of the employer's total workforce must be EEA nationals
- Applies to both GEP and CSEP
- Applies to all business sizes — no exemption for small employers
- Two exceptions: (1) the non-EEA national will be the sole employee of the business, or (2) the employer is an Enterprise Ireland or IDA Ireland-backed start-up registered within the previous 2 years with a formal EI/IDA letter of support
- Calculated by counting all employees (full-time and part-time equally) across all locations
- Assessed on the date of application submission, not permit issue date

**The Labour Market Needs Test (LMNT)**
- Required for all General Employment Permit applications
- Mandatory advertising period: 28 days minimum
- Required advertising platforms: Jobs Ireland (DSP Employment Services/EURES) and at least one additional online platform
- Employer must document all applications received and reasons for not progressing any Irish or EEA candidates
- If the 50/50 ratio drops during the LMNT period, the full 28-day test must be rerun once the ratio recovers

**Permit Quotas (2026)**
- 1,134 permits allocated for healthcare support workers
- 1,000 permits for meat processing operatives
- 850 permits for dairy farm assistants

## How CA Recruitment Works

The process for Irish employers has seven steps:

1. **Free Consultation** — CA Recruitment assesses the role, permit eligibility, current workforce, and whether overseas recruitment is the right option. No obligation or fee.
2. **Candidate Sourcing and Vetting** — CA Recruitment sources candidates from the Philippines through an established network. Employers receive a shortlist of pre-screened candidates and conduct video interviews.
3. **Labour Market Needs Test** (GEP applications only) — CA Recruitment manages the 28-day advertising on Jobs Ireland and a second platform, records all applications, and produces the documentation DETE requires.
4. **Permit Application Submitted** — CA Recruitment prepares all documentation and submits through DETE's Employment Permits Online System (EPOS) on the employer's behalf.
5. **DETE Processing** — CA Recruitment monitors progress and handles any DETE requests for additional information.
6. **Visa and Pre-Departure** — Once the permit is approved, CA Recruitment guides the worker through their Irish visa application and pre-departure preparation.
7. **Arrival and First 90 Days** — CA Recruitment supports onboarding and checks in with both employer and worker. Free replacement if the placement does not work out within 90 days.

## Employment Permit Guides

- [How to Hire Overseas Care Assistants in Ireland](https://carecruitment.com/blog/hire-overseas-care-assistants-ireland/index.md): GEP process for nursing homes and homecare — which roles qualify (Care Assistant, Healthcare Support Worker), LMNT requirements, HIQA compliance, salary thresholds, DETE timelines, why Filipino care workers
- [Hiring Non-EU Workers for Your Irish Business: A Plain-English Guide](https://carecruitment.com/blog/hiring-non-eu-workers-ireland-guide/index.md): Full explainer for employers new to overseas hiring — permit types, LMNT, 50/50 rule, application process, employer obligations post-arrival
- [GEP vs CSEP: Which Irish Employment Permit Does Your Business Need?](https://carecruitment.com/blog/gep-vs-csep-which-permit/index.md): Side-by-side comparison of the General Employment Permit and Critical Skills Employment Permit — salary thresholds, LMNT requirement, processing times, visa pathway differences
- [The 50:50 Rule for Employment Permits in Ireland](https://carecruitment.com/blog/50-50-rule-irish-employment-permits/index.md): How the 50/50 EEA workforce ratio is calculated, when it applies, the two exceptions, what happens if the ratio falls during the LMNT period, and how to stay compliant
- [Hospitality Work Permits Ireland 2026](https://carecruitment.com/blog/hospitality-work-permits-ireland-2026/index.md): Permit routes for chefs and hospitality roles — eligible grades, experience requirements, salary thresholds, timeline for hospitality placements
- [Is Ireland Still Recruiting Care Workers from Overseas?](https://carecruitment.com/blog/is-ireland-recruiting-care-workers-overseas/index.md): Current state of overseas healthcare recruitment in Ireland — DETE quota data, HSE demand, nursing home staffing crisis context

## Sector Pages

- [Care and Social Services](https://carecruitment.com/sectors/care-social-services/index.md): Roles filled — care workers, support workers, social care workers, community support workers, day service workers, relief care workers. GEP permit, 3–5 month timeline.
- [Healthcare](https://carecruitment.com/sectors/healthcare/index.md): Healthcare assistants and home support workers (GEP); registered nurses may qualify for CSEP. NMBI registration pathway for Filipino nurses explained.
- [Agriculture](https://carecruitment.com/sectors/agriculture/index.md): Dairy farm operatives, pig unit workers, horticulture workers, mushroom pickers, harvest workers, general farm hands. GEP permit, 5–8 month timeline.
- [Construction](https://carecruitment.com/sectors/construction/index.md): General construction workers, steel fixers, scaffolders, groundworkers, plant operators. GEP permit, 5–8 month timeline.
- [Hospitality](https://carecruitment.com/sectors/hospitality/index.md): Chefs (Executive, Head, Sous, Chef de Partie, Commis). GEP permit. Chef candidates must meet DETE minimum experience requirements (5 years for senior grades, 2 years for junior grades, outside fast-food).
- [Logistics and Warehousing](https://carecruitment.com/sectors/logistics-warehousing/index.md): Warehouse operatives, forklift drivers, delivery drivers, logistics coordinators.
- [Manufacturing](https://carecruitment.com/sectors/manufacturing/index.md): Production operatives, machine operators, quality control, assembly workers.
- [Retail](https://carecruitment.com/sectors/retail/index.md): Retail assistants, supervisors, stock room staff.
- [Accounting and Finance](https://carecruitment.com/sectors/accounting-finance/index.md): Accounts assistants, bookkeepers, payroll administrators, financial controllers.

## Key Questions Answered

**Can an Irish employer hire a worker from the Philippines?**
Yes. Irish employers can hire Filipino workers under the DETE General Employment Permit (for most roles) or Critical Skills Employment Permit (for high-skill shortage roles). The employer must meet the 50/50 EEA workforce ratio, pass the Labour Market Needs Test (GEP only), pay at least the minimum salary for the permit type, and cover the €1,000 government permit fee. It is a criminal offence to pass the permit cost to the worker.

**How long does it take to hire a Filipino worker for an Irish business?**
For a General Employment Permit: about 6 to 8 months from first consultation to the worker starting, across all sectors. This includes the mandatory 28-day Labour Market Needs Test and 8 to 12 weeks of DETE processing, plus the D-visa and travel from the Philippines. A Critical Skills Employment Permit does not require the Labour Market Needs Test, which can shorten the process.

**What is the minimum salary for a work permit in Ireland?**
The General Employment Permit standard minimum is €36,605 per year. Shortage occupation rates apply for specific roles: €32,691 for healthcare support workers (care assistants, home carers) and horticulture workers. The Critical Skills Employment Permit minimum is €40,904 for listed occupations, or €68,911 for occupations not on the CSEP list. All permit holders must also receive at least the National Minimum Wage (€14.15/hour as of January 2026).

**Does the Labour Market Needs Test take 28 days?**
Yes. The LMNT requires a minimum of 28 days of advertising on Jobs Ireland (DSP Employment Services/EURES network) and at least one additional online platform before a GEP application can be submitted. The employer must document all applications received and provide reasons for not progressing any Irish or EEA candidates.

**What is the 50/50 rule for Irish work permits?**
At least 50% of the employer's total workforce must be EEA nationals at the time of any employment permit application. This applies to all sizes of business and to both GEP and CSEP. The two exceptions are: (1) the non-EEA worker will be the only employee, or (2) the employer is an EI/IDA-backed start-up within 2 years with a formal support letter.

**What does CA Recruitment charge?**
No placement fee is charged until the worker starts. CA Recruitment charges a placement fee on successful placement only. A 90-day free replacement guarantee applies to all placements. The government permit fee (€1,000 for most GEPs) is paid directly by the employer to DETE.

**Can an employer hire a care assistant from overseas in Ireland?**
Yes. Care assistants and healthcare support workers are on DETE's eligible occupations list under SI 64/2025, with 1,134 permits allocated in 2026. The minimum salary is €32,691 (the shortage occupation rate). The employer applies for a General Employment Permit. Senior Care Worker is on the ineligible list for standard nursing homes and home care operators — the correct permit title is Care Assistant or Healthcare Support Worker. An exception applies for disability services run by Section 38 or Section 39 HSE-funded organisations.
